title: 3Commas Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026)

meta_description: 3Commas Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026). 3Commas (4.1★, Free). Step-by-step setup, features walkthrough, and pro tips. Updated 2026.
excerpt: Complete guide to setting up and using 3Commas — from account creation to pro-level tips.
content:
What is 3Commas?

3Commas is a leading crypto trading bot platform founded in 2017 and based in Tallinn, Estonia, that automates trading across 20+ cryptocurrency exchanges. With a 4.1/5 rating and pricing starting at free with pro plans up to $99/mo, it serves traders who want to automate DCA (dollar-cost averaging), grid trading, and signal-based strategies without manual execution. The platform combines multiple bot types, AI-assisted configuration, backtesting, and paper trading into one interface, making it ideal for both newcomers exploring automation and experienced traders managing complex portfolios. If you're looking to scale your crypto trading without constantly staring at charts, 3Commas removes the friction between strategy and execution.

How to Create Your 3Commas Account

Getting started with 3Commas takes about 10-15 minutes from sign-up to your first bot. Here's the exact process:

  • Go to 3commas.io and click "Sign Up" — You'll see options to register with email, Google, or Metamask. Email registration is fastest.
  • Verify your email address — Check your inbox for a verification link. This step typically takes under a minute.
  • Complete your profile information — Enter your username, password (strong one required), and optionally your trading experience level. This helps 3Commas tailor the onboarding experience.
  • Enable two-factor authentication (2FA) — Strongly recommended. You can use authenticator apps like Google Authenticator or Authy. Save your backup codes in a secure location.
  • Connect your exchange API keys — This is where 3Commas gains permission to execute trades on your behalf. Go to Settings → Connect Exchanges and choose your primary exchange (Binance, Coinbase Pro, FTX alternatives, etc.). You'll copy API keys from your exchange account into 3Commas. Ensure your API keys have trading permissions enabled but NOT withdrawal permissions for security.
  • Select your subscription plan — Choose between Free, Starter ($29/mo), Advanced ($49/mo), or Pro ($99/mo) based on how many bots you want to run.
  • Review account security settings — Confirm withdrawal address whitelist, IP restrictions, and session timeout preferences.

The entire process is straightforward, though connecting API keys requires some attention to detail. Most new users finish in under 20 minutes. One critical note: never share API keys in screenshots or via email — 3Commas stores them encrypted on their servers, but if compromised at the exchange level, your funds are at risk.

Setting Up 3Commas for the First Time

After account creation, you'll land on the Dashboard — a grid of widgets showing portfolio performance, bot activity, and market data. Here's how to configure it for productive trading:

Dashboard Customization: Right-click any widget to rearrange or remove it. The most important widgets are Bot Performance (showing profit/loss by bot), Portfolio Overview (your total holdings), and Account Balance. Remove widgets you won't use frequently to reduce clutter.

Connect All Your Exchanges: In Settings → Exchanges, add every exchange where you want to run bots. You can connect Binance, Bybit, OKX, Crypto.com, and 18+ others simultaneously. This flexibility lets you run different bot strategies on different exchanges based on liquidity and fees.

Set Your Base Currency and Timezone: Under Account Settings, select your home currency (USD, EUR, GBP, etc.) for profit calculations and your timezone so alerts and scheduled bots trigger at the right times.

Configure Notifications: Go to Notifications and enable alerts for bot completions, errors, and significant price movements. You can receive these via email, Telegram (recommended for real-time alerts), or both.

Review API Key Permissions: Return to each exchange and verify that your API keys have only the permissions 3Commas needs: spot trading (not margin trading unless you specifically want it) and no withdrawal access. This prevents catastrophic loss if 3Commas' systems were compromised.

Explore the Bot Templates: Before creating your first bot, visit Templates in the sidebar. 3Commas provides pre-built strategies for DCA, grid trading, and signal-following. These templates give you a head start and show best-practice configurations.

Essential Features You Should Know

1. DCA Bots (Dollar-Cost Averaging): These bots buy a crypto asset at fixed intervals regardless of price, reducing the impact of market timing mistakes. You set the investment amount (e.g., $50 every 3 days), max price, and take-profit level. DCA bots are ideal for traders who want consistent accumulation during downturns — let the bot handle buying while you ignore price swings.

2. Grid Bots: Grid bots buy and sell within a price range, capturing profits on every fluctuation. You define the price range, number of grid lines, and investment. If Bitcoin ranges from $40,000 to $42,000, a grid bot might place 10 buy orders between those levels and automatically sell when price bounces. This is perfect for sideways markets where volatility is high but direction is uncertain.

3. Signal-Based Bots: Connect external trading signals (from TradingView, other platforms, or your own alerts) and 3Commas automatically executes trades. Configure entry conditions, stop-loss, and take-profit levels once, then let signals trigger trades 24/7 without monitoring.

4. AI Assistant for Bot Configuration: Instead of manually tweaking bot parameters, describe your strategy in plain language and 3Commas' AI recommends settings. For example, "I want to buy Bitcoin on dips with a $100 investment" generates a pre-configured bot. This significantly cuts setup time for traders unfamiliar with the platform.

5. Backtesting Engine: Test your bot strategy against 6+ years of historical data before deploying real money. Run 100 different parameter combinations in seconds and compare win rates, average profit, and max drawdown. Critical for avoiding strategies that sound good but fail in live conditions.

6. Paper Trading (Demo Mode): Run bots with real market data but zero actual trades or money at risk. Perfect for learning the platform or validating a new strategy. Paper trading executes at exactly the same timing as live trading, so results are realistic.

7. Performance Analytics Dashboard: View detailed stats for each bot: total profit/loss, number of trades, win rate, average trade duration, and drawdown metrics. Filter by date range, exchange, and bot type to isolate which strategies are working and which need adjustment.

3Commas Pricing: Which Plan Should You Choose?

Free Tier ($0/month): One active bot, up to 10 market trades daily, basic backtesting, no signal bots, limited to 4 connected exchanges. Best for absolute beginners testing the platform with small stakes. You get full access to DCA and grid bot templates, so you're not severely limited on strategy types — just quantity of simultaneous bots.

Starter Plan ($29/month): Three active bots, 150 market trades daily, full backtesting, signal bot support, up to 6 connected exchanges. Recommended for traders running 2-3 strategies simultaneously. Most intermediate traders find this tier sufficient for portfolio management across 1-2 exchanges.

Advanced Plan ($49/month): 10 active bots, 500 market trades daily, advanced backtesting features, unlimited signal bots, up to 10 exchanges. This is where most serious traders settle — enough bot capacity to diversify strategies without redundant subscriptions.

Pro Plan ($99/month): 20 active bots, unlimited trades, advanced AI features, VIP support, 20 connected exchanges. Only necessary if you're running an operations-heavy trading business with dozens of strategies or managing funds for others.

Plan Recommendation by Trader Type: Beginners start Free and upgrade to Starter ($29) after 2-4 weeks once you understand which strategies work for your risk tolerance. Intermediate traders (6+ months experience) jump straight to Advanced ($49) to avoid hitting bot limits. Professional traders or fund managers use Pro ($99) for unlimited scaling. Don't overpay — most profitable traders operate from Advanced because bot quantity rarely matters as much as strategy quality.

Pro Tips for Getting the Most Out of 3Commas

1. Use Telegram for Real-Time Alerts, Not Email: Email notifications arrive 2-5 minutes late on 3Commas. Connect Telegram in Settings → Notifications and enable trade completion, error, and price alerts. You'll get instant pushes on your phone when anything happens, letting you react to failures immediately instead of discovering them hours later.

2. Start with Paper Trading on Every New Strategy: Before deploying $500 to a new bot, run it in paper trading mode for 1-2 weeks. This reveals parameter mistakes (stop-loss too tight, grid spacing too wide, etc.) without costing actual money. Traders who skip this step consistently burn money on poorly-tuned bots.

3. Backtest Across Multiple Market Conditions: Test your bot strategy on 2017-2018 (crypto winter), 2020-2021 (bull run), and current data. A DCA strategy might look great on bull runs but devastating in downturns. 3Commas backtesting lets you test any 6-year date range — use this to stress-test rather than cherry-picking profitable years.

4. Set Take-Profit Targets Conservatively: New traders often set grid bot take-profit at 2-5%, expecting constant profit extraction. In low-volatility environments, that grid sits idle for weeks. More effective: 0.3-0.7% per trade on grid bots. This generates more frequent small wins rather than betting on large price swings.

5. Monitor Drawdown Limits Like a Risk Manager: 3Commas lets you set max drawdown — the largest loss a bot can take before stopping. For DCA bots accumulating long-term, set drawdown 20-30% below your entry. For grid bots, set 5-10%. Protect yourself by hardcoding when bots must stop, not hoping they'll recover.

6. Stagger Your Bot Start Times Across Exchanges: If you run the same DCA strategy on Binance and Bybit, offset their buy schedules by a few hours. This diversifies entry points across time, reducing the risk that both bots buy right before a sharp drop.

7. Review Bot Performance Weekly: Every Sunday, export your bot performance data (Settings → Export) and analyze which bots earned money and which lost. Kill underperforming bots quickly — sunk-cost fallacy makes traders let losing bots run for months hoping they'll turn around. Data beats hope.

Common 3Commas Issues and How to Fix Them

API Connection Drops (Bot Stops Executing Trades): If your bot suddenly stops working, the first cause is usually an exchange outage or API rate-limit breach. Go to Settings → Exchanges and click "Test Connection" for each one. If the test fails, check your exchange's status page. If it passes but bots still don't execute, log into your exchange directly and verify API key permissions haven't been accidentally revoked. 3Commas support can investigate further, but 99% of connection issues resolve by reconnecting API keys.

Backtesting Results Don't Match Live Trading Performance: Backtesting assumes perfect order fills at the OHLCV price data. Live trading faces slippage, liquidity gaps, and partial fills. Your bot likely performs 5-15% worse live than backtest results predict, especially on smaller exchanges with less liquidity. Adjust your expectations downward and use live performance to iterate, not backtesting alone.

High Fees Eroding Profits: Each exchange charges different trading fees. Binance charges 0.1% (lower with BNB holdings), while Crypto.com charges 0.4% for spot trades. A grid bot making 50 trades monthly on Crypto.com loses 2% to fees before profit takes. Solution: run high-frequency strategies (grid, signal) on Binance; use low-frequency strategies (DCA) on higher-fee exchanges where fee impact matters less.

"Not Enough Balance" Errors on Grid Bots: Grid bots pre-allocate funds across all price levels upfront. If you set a $1,000 grid with 10 levels, 3Commas reserves $1,000 immediately, even if only $300 is used initially. Solution: reduce grid size or lower investment amount. Alternatively, use "Fund" option to add balance without closing the bot.

Is 3Commas Worth It? Our Verdict

3Commas is genuinely the best crypto bot platform for traders who value breadth over depth, offering DCA, grid, and signal bots across 20+ exchanges with AI-assisted setup and solid backtesting. The 4.1/5 rating reflects strong functionality, though the 2022 security breach and $99 pro pricing mean it's not frictionless for everyone. Start free, graduate to Starter ($29/mo) once you're confident, and stick with Advanced ($49/mo) for most strategies — you'll recoup fees within weeks if your bots are tuned properly. Skip it entirely if you trade stocks or forex; consider it essential if you're serious about crypto automation.

Compare 3Commas to Cryptohopper (simpler UI, fewer exchanges) and Gunbot (more technical, cheaper long-term). Or check our full 3Commas review for deeper analysis on security, profitability, and feature-by-feature breakdowns.
